who was the first steam boat operator who was the first steam boat operator the first steam boat operator is widely considered to be robert fulton, an american engineer and inventor. on august 17, 1807, fulton successfully operated the first commercial steamboat, the clermont, on a voyage from new york city to albany, a distance of 150 miles, in just 32 hours. this groundbreaking achievement marked the beginning of a new era in transportation, revolutionizing the way people and goods were moved on water. fulton's innovative design and perseverance made him a pioneer in the field of steam navigation, and his legacy paved the way for the development of modern steamboats and ships that transformed global trade and commerce.
